meta.title: Alex Chen - Frontend Architect
meta.description: Senior frontend engineer building performant, accessible interfaces at scale

sections.headline: PERFORMANCE. ACCESSIBILITY. SCALE.
sections.subheadline: I build frontend systems that handle millions of users. Currently architecting UI infrastructure at Stripe. Previously led frontend performance at Vercel.

sections.heading: SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE
sections.body: My approach to frontend engineering is systematic: measure everything, optimize what matters, document decisions. I've built component libraries used by 500+ developers, reduced page load times by 60% for Fortune 500 companies, and contributed core features to Next.js.

items.title: STRIPE DASHBOARD
items.description: Led frontend redesign reducing load times by 60%. Implemented design system used across 50+ products. 99.99% uptime.

items.title: VERCEL ANALYTICS
items.description: Real-time dashboard processing 10M+ events daily. Sub-100ms query times. Edge-based architecture.

items.title: COMPONENT LIBRARY
items.description: Design system used by 500+ developers. 98% test coverage. Comprehensive documentation. Zero breaking changes.

items.title: E-COMMERCE PLATFORM
items.description: Headless architecture handling 10K+ daily orders. 2-second page loads. 99.9% uptime. GraphQL API.

items.title: PERFORMANCE MONITORING
items.description: Custom RUM dashboard tracking Core Web Vitals. Automated regression detection. 40% performance improvement.

items.title: NEXT.JS CONTRIBUTIONS
items.description: 47 merged PRs to core. Server components optimization. Image optimization improvements. Cache strategy work.

items.quote: Alex's systematic approach to performance optimization reduced our page load times by 60%. The impact on conversion was immediate and measurable.
items.name: Sarah Miller

items.quote: The component library Alex architected transformed our development velocity. 500+ developers now ship with confidence and consistency.
items.name: James Park

items.quote: Alex's contributions to Next.js core have benefited the entire ecosystem. His work on server components is foundational.
items.name: Guillermo Rauch

sections.heading: INITIATE CONTACT
sections.subheading: Available for consulting and full-time roles
